The Brahmaputra river originates in Tibet and flows through Arunachal Pradesh and Assam. The Ganges is the longest river in India and is considered holy by millions. The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are a union territory of India located at the juncture of the Bay of Bengal and the Andaman Sea. Chilika Lake in Odisha is the largest coastal lagoon in India and the second largest in the world. Majuli in Assam is the largest river island in the world, located in the Brahmaputra River. The Godavari is the second longest river in India after the Ganga and is often called the Dakshin Ganga. Sundarbans is the largest mangrove forest in the world, located in the delta region of the Bay of Bengal. The Narmada and Tapti are the major west-flowing rivers in peninsular India. India is the seventh-largest country by total area in the world. The Himalayas, the world's highest mountain range, form the northern border of the country. The Thar Desert, also known as the Great Indian Desert, forms a natural boundary between India and Pakistan. The Aravalli Range is one of the oldest fold mountain systems in the world. The Malabar Coast in southwestern India receives the first heavy rains of the southwest monsoon. Lakshadweep is a tropical archipelago of 36 atolls and coral reefs in the Laccadive Sea. The Deccan Plateau makes up the majority of the southern part of the country. It is bounded by the Indian Ocean on the south, the Arabian Sea on the southwest, and the Bay of Bengal on the southeast.
